interactions.description: Both are Arishta preparations; concurrent use may increase alcohol content intake.
interactions.recommendation: Space doses at least 2 hours apart.

interactions.description: Both contain Guggulu resin; combined use may cause excessive purgation.
interactions.recommendation: Do not prescribe together. Choose one based on primary dosha involvement.

interactions.description: Both contain Haritaki. Combined use increases laxative effect.
interactions.recommendation: Reduce dosage of one if used together. Monitor bowel movements.

interactions.description: Arogyavardhini contains metallic bhasma; Kamdudha also has mineral content. Cumulative heavy metal exposure risk.
interactions.recommendation: Limit duration to 2 weeks when co-prescribed. Monitor liver function.

interactions.description: Both have diuretic action. May cause excessive fluid loss.
interactions.recommendation: Ensure adequate hydration. Monitor electrolytes in elderly patients.

interactions.description: Kutajarishta is anti-diarrheal while Triphala is laxative. Opposing actions.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. Prescribe sequentially based on symptom phase.

interactions.description: Both are Rasayana (rejuvenation) formulations. Generally safe combination.
interactions.recommendation: Safe to co-prescribe. Monitor for excessive sedation.

interactions.description: Same formulation in different dosage forms. Risk of double dosing.
interactions.recommendation: Prescribe only one form. Do not combine.

interactions.description: Both are Guggulu preparations with overlapping ingredients.
interactions.recommendation: Choose one based on primary indication. Kaishore for Pitta-Rakta, Yogaraja for Vata.

interactions.description: Avipattikar reduces acidity while Lavanbhaskar increases digestive fire. May partially counteract.
interactions.recommendation: Use Avipattikar for hyperacidity (Amlapitta) and Lavanbhaskar for low Agni. Avoid concurrent use.

arogyavardhini vati.pediatric_alternative: Use Kumaryasava or other non-mineral alternatives for children.

yogaraja guggulu.pediatric_alternative: Use Rasnadi Kashaya for children under 8.

chandraprabha vati.pediatric_alternative: Use Punarnava Mandur for children under 10.

kamdudha rasa.pediatric_alternative: Use Shatavari Ghrita for children under 6.
